title: Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus
root_mechanism: Visceral adiposity → hepatic and muscle insulin resistance → pancreatic beta-cell stress. Excess ectopic fat triggers chronic low-grade inflammation and blunts the IRS-1/PI3K/GLUT4 signalling cascade, forcing hyperinsulinaemia until beta-cell exhaustion leads to overt hyperglycaemia.

interventions.intervention: Sunlight exposure (10–15 min) + Cold shower (3 min)
interventions.physiological_effect: Resets circadian clock; norepinephrine surge activates brown adipose tissue → lowers fasting glucose

interventions.intervention: Dry brushing (5 min) + Abhyanga — warm sesame oil massage (10 min)
interventions.physiological_effect: Cutaneous lymphatic stimulation; skin mechanoreceptors trigger vagal activation → lowers cortisol

interventions.intervention: Nadi Shodhana — alternate nostril breathing (5 min / 10 rounds)
interventions.physiological_effect: Modulates hypothalamic lateralisation → balances sympatho-vagal tone → reduces sympathetic glucagon release

interventions.intervention: Fast until 11 AM — 14–16 h overnight fast
interventions.physiological_effect: Depletes liver glycogen; lowers basal insulin; activates AMPK → triggers autophagy and fat oxidation

interventions.intervention: Abdominal mud pack — room temperature clay (20 min)
interventions.physiological_effect: Clay draws heat from visceral region; increases local blood flow and lipolysis; mild reflex parasympathetic activation

interventions.intervention: Apple cider vinegar — 2 tbsp in water (10 min before meal)
interventions.physiological_effect: Acetic acid inhibits amylase, slows gastric emptying → natural GLP-1 mimic → blunts postprandial glucose spike

interventions.intervention: Eat in order: Protein → Fibre → Carbs (small portion)
interventions.physiological_effect: Protein stimulates incretin release; fibre forms viscous gel slowing glucose absorption; carbs enter an already-buffered gut

interventions.intervention: Walk (15 min) or standing calf raises (3 × 20 reps)
interventions.physiological_effect: Muscle contraction triggers GLUT4 vesicle translocation → insulin-independent glucose uptake

interventions.intervention: Cold pack on upper abdomen — cloth between skin and pack (20 min)
interventions.physiological_effect: Local cold → norepinephrine → beta-3 adrenergic activation on omental adipocytes → direct visceral lipolysis
Twisting postures compress pancreas/liver → improve microcirculation and insulin secretion; inversions reduce cortisol

interventions.intervention: Contrast foot bath alternating hot/cold (10 min) + Cold spinal pack T6–T10 (15 min)
interventions.physiological_effect: Vascular pumping via alternating vasodilation/vasoconstriction; cold spinal pack reduces splanchnic sympathetic outflow
Prevents late insulin spike; aligns with circadian insulin sensitivity nadir; forces overnight fat oxidation
Transdermal magnesium absorption → cofactor for insulin receptor tyrosine kinase → improves receptor signalling
Hours 12–14 deplete hepatic glycogen; fatty acid beta-oxidation dominates; ketone bodies reduce hunger hormones

practices.maintenance_dose: 30 min walk or 20 min yoga, 6 × / week
Only if waist increases OR fasting glucose > 105 mg/dL for 3 consecutive days
Only if HbA1c rises > 6.5% — clinician supervised only

relapse_rules.action: Return to Phase 2 + daily cold pack
Phase 1 for 14 days, then Phase 2 for 4 weeks

fasting_rules.physiological_effect: Depletes liver glycogen; activates AMPK, autophagy and fat oxidation
Aligns food intake with peak insulin sensitivity (morning–afternoon circadian peak)
Protein triggers early GLP-1 release; fibre slows glucose absorption; carbs arrive into a buffered gut — blunts postprandial spike

fasting_rules.specification: 2 tbsp in water — 10 min before lunch
fasting_rules.physiological_effect: Acetic acid inhibits amylase and slows gastric emptying — natural GLP-1 mimic
Prevents late insulin spike; forces overnight fat oxidation aligned with circadian nadir
GLUT4 translocation — insulin-independent skeletal muscle glucose uptake

phase_macros.rationale: Aggressive glycogen depletion; ketosis induction; lower basal insulin
Sustained fat adaptation; beta-cell rest and recovery
Long-term metabolic flexibility; prevent visceral fat regain

items: Millets — ragi, jowar, bajra (small portion at lunch only, never at dinner)
Idli/dosa in small amounts (Phase 2–3 only) — for gut microbiome support
Breakfast cereals — hidden sugars and trans fats
All alcohol — causes hypoglycaemia (especially with medications), impairs liver gluconeogenesis regulation
Any carbohydrate after 6 PM — disrupts circadian insulin rhythm, promotes fat storage
More than 1 serving/day of any fruit — fructose directly raises triglycerides and worsens fatty liver

meals.what_to_eat: Fast — plain water or jeera water only
meals.physiological_rationale: Maintains overnight fasting state; no insulin spike

meals.what_to_eat: Amla juice (30 mL) or methi seed water (soaked overnight seeds strained out)
meals.physiological_rationale: Amla = vitamin C + tannins that improve insulin sensitivity; methi reduces gastric emptying

meals.what_to_eat: 2–3 eggs (boiled/scrambled in coconut oil) + 1 cup sautéed vegetables (spinach, broccoli) + small salad with lemon dressing
meals.physiological_rationale: High protein + fibre opens the eating window; low carb keeps insulin low

meals.what_to_eat: 15-min walk or calf raises immediately after
meals.physiological_rationale: GLUT4 translocation — glucose uptake without insulin

meals.what_to_eat: ACV water → Protein (100 g chicken/fish/dal) → Salad with fibre (cucumber, lettuce, flaxseed) → Small portion millet or brown rice (30–50 g cooked, Phase 2 only)
meals.physiological_rationale: Strict meal sequence to flatten glucose curve; ACV taken 10 min prior

meals.what_to_eat: Handful of walnuts/almonds + 1 cup green tea (no sugar) OR 1 cup buttermilk with roasted jeera
meals.physiological_rationale: Healthy fat snack; buttermilk provides gut-friendly Lactobacillus

meals.what_to_eat: 1 cup dal/lentil soup + 2 eggs or paneer (50 g) + non-starchy stir-fried vegetables (no roti, rice or bread)
meals.physiological_rationale: Protein + fat only — prevents nocturnal insulin spike; supports fat oxidation during sleep

meals.what_to_eat: Fast begins — water, herbal tea (tulsi, cinnamon, ginger) allowed
meals.physiological_rationale: Overnight fast restarts; no calories until 11 AM next day

functional_foods.name: Bitter gourd (Karela) juice
functional_foods.dose_timing: 50–100 mL fresh juice on empty stomach, 3 × /week
functional_foods.mechanism_of_action: Charantin and polypeptide-P act as phytoinsulin; activates AMPK; inhibits intestinal glucose uptake

functional_foods.name: Fenugreek (Methi) seeds
functional_foods.dose_timing: 1 tsp soaked overnight in water — eat seeds + drink water at 7 AM
functional_foods.mechanism_of_action: Soluble fibre (galactomannan) slows gastric emptying; 4-hydroxyisoleucine directly stimulates insulin secretion

functional_foods.name: Amla (Indian gooseberry)
functional_foods.dose_timing: 30 mL juice or 1 fresh fruit daily — morning
functional_foods.mechanism_of_action: Chromium content improves insulin receptor binding; polyphenols reduce oxidative stress on beta cells

functional_foods.name: Cinnamon (Dalchini)
functional_foods.dose_timing: ½ tsp powder in warm water or food daily
functional_foods.mechanism_of_action: Methylhydroxychalcone polymer (MHCP) mimics insulin; enhances GLUT4 expression in muscle cells

functional_foods.name: Turmeric (Haldi)
functional_foods.dose_timing: 1 tsp in warm water or milk (no sugar) — evening
functional_foods.mechanism_of_action: Curcumin reduces NF-kB inflammation; protects beta cells from cytokine-mediated apoptosis

functional_foods.name: Jamun seed powder
functional_foods.dose_timing: 1 tsp powder with water, twice daily before meals
functional_foods.mechanism_of_action: Jamboline and jambosine slow starch-to-sugar conversion; reduces post-meal glucose spikes

functional_foods.name: Gurmar (Gymnema sylvestre)
functional_foods.dose_timing: 400 mg standardised extract with meals — prescribed by clinician
functional_foods.mechanism_of_action: Gymnemic acids block intestinal glucose absorption and reduce sweet taste sensation; promotes beta-cell regeneration

functional_foods.name: Jeera (Cumin) water
functional_foods.dose_timing: 1 tsp seeds boiled in 2 cups water — drink warm, morning or with meals
functional_foods.mechanism_of_action: Thymoquinone in cumin improves insulin sensitivity; reduces hepatic glucose production

asanas.physiological_effect: Vagal activation → suppresses hepatic glucose output
Spinal mobilisation → improves pancreatic autonomic nerve input
Mechanical compression of pancreas → increases local blood flow and insulin secretion
Venous return improvement; reduces sympathetic tone → lowers cortisol
Massages abdominal viscera → improves splanchnic circulation
Mechanical thyroid stimulation → improves basal metabolic rate
Parasympathetic dominance → resets HPA axis, lowers cortisol
Kapalabhati massages pancreas via abdominal contractions; Nadi Shodhana improves dynamic insulin response

red_flags.action: Rule out Diabetic Ketoacidosis (DKA) — halt fasting immediately.
Especially on insulin or sulfonylureas — stop fasting, adjust medications urgently.
Rule out LADA (Latent Autoimmune Diabetes in Adults) or occult malignancy.

red_flags.sign: New vision changes, floaters or sudden visual loss
red_flags.action: Rule out retinal haemorrhage — urgent ophthalmology referral.

red_flags.sign: Foot ulcer, numbness or non-healing wound
red_flags.action: Urgent wound care assessment — do not apply cold packs locally.

clinical_disclaimer: This protocol is designed to be implemented under the supervision of a qualified BNYS physician. Patients on antidiabetic medications (particularly insulin or sulfonylureas) must have drug doses reviewed before initiating fasting interventions to prevent hypoglycaemia. This document is for clinical reference only and does not substitute for individualised medical assessment.
